claim: AWS Agent Core's policy engine processes thousands of requests per second, testing policies in milliseconds before allowing agent tool access
actionable: Use Agent Core policies to gate agent access to tools; define restrictions in natural language which auto-compile to code
source_title: AWS just made some MASSIVE Announcements (AgentCore)

claim: Agent Core evaluations can test correctness, helpfulness, conciseness, instruction following, faithfulness, and refusal simultaneously
actionable: Use Agent Core evals to establish multi-dimensional agent baselines; evaluate continuously as you deploy changes
source_title: AWS just made some MASSIVE Announcements (AgentCore)

claim: Agent Core episodic memory allows agents to learn from successes and failures across interactions, propagating patterns to all future use
actionable: Enable episodic memory in Agent Core to let agents learn from mistakes; use evaluations to measure if memory improves performance
source_title: AWS just made some MASSIVE Announcements (AgentCore)

claim: 95% of AI pilots inside enterprises fail; Agent Core's policy, evals, and memory features address the trust and control problems causing failures
actionable: When deploying agents to enterprise, build governance (policies), measurement (evals), and learning (memory) from day one
source_title: AWS just made some MASSIVE Announcements (AgentCore)
